content:
1. Pattern recognition was instant, but took a bit longer to realise the "X".
2. Took around 3min for me.

n = int(input())  # Note: 0 < n ≤ 20
print("*" * (2 * n + 1))
for i in range(0, 10, n):
    print("*" + "*".join(str(x % 10) if x <= 10 else "X" for x in range(i + 1, i + n + 1)) + "*")
    print("*" * (2 * n + 1))

This is a nice Reverse clash! Can't comment much about difficulty (I don't think you'd trust my judgement on this :D), but it should be solvable within 15min. Hope this helps :)

Really enjoyed this challenge!
1. around 30 seconds
2. yes, solved in 3 minutes

here is my solution code for any future references:
import sys
import math
n = int(input())  
bar = n*2+1
print(bar*"*")
for x in range(math.ceil(10/n)):
    for j in range(n):
        if x*n+j+1 <= 10:
            print("*" + str((x*n+j+1) %10),end="")
        else:
            print("*X",end="")
    print("*")
    print(bar*"*")

Can 4 more people try to solve this clash (without copying any solutions below). Then can you comment your answers to these 2 questions below?
1. How fast did you recognize the pattern?
2. Did you solve it in under 15 minutes? (or could you even solve it?)

Sorry; this is my first reverse puzzle, so I'm really sensitive about the difficulty of this. I don't want this approved if you guys think this is too hard. Thanks again for your feedback!

Hi
3. managed it - was ok for me - still an early clasher but  I have already seen clashes way 'tighter' I couldn't finish (e.g printing words crossed at earliest common vowel)- posting my initial sol passing the tests as a hint for how others tackle this

m=n=int(input())
while m<10:m+=n
l=m//n
s=[*map(str,range(1,10))]+['0']+['X']*(m-10)
b=(2*n+1)*'*'
print(b)
for i in range(l):
    print('*'+'*'.join(s[i*n:(i+1)*n])+'*')
    print(b)

Could you guys direct any feedback to these concerns below? Thank you so much!
1. How can I add the constraint of n>0 as a comment to the input.
(ex. n=int(input()) # 0<n≤20)
2. Should my WRITE statement just be something like "write answer," or is the one I have okay?
3. BIG ONE: Try to solve this clash (without copying my solution). If I were to assume, the pattern is probably easily recognizable, but the programming might take a while. Did it take you less than 15 minutes? Could you even solve it? I just want to make sure this puzzle is novice-medium difficulty.
4. If there is anything else that I am missing (like edge test cases), feel free to point it out!

Here are my answers to some of your questions.

Q1. You can use the INPUT keyword. Refer to the explanation here: ( https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/stub-generator ) .

Q2. The write statement you have is fine.

Q4. You've done a good job in identifying all the edge test cases. However, I don't think the test case with n = 20 is an edge case (hitting the upper bound of the constraint doesn't necessarily make it one). Please change the value of n in one of the n = 20 cases to another value, e.g. 19.

Also, although it's not required for the Reverse mode, please format "n" as a [[variable]] in the Constraints section 😉

Thanks.
I wrote 'early clasher' but I meant recent clasher
Nice! Thanks for the feedback @Vincent18! That should mean that this isn't a guaranteed sub-minute puzzle for clash of code gurus/sweats, which is what I want. Glad you liked it :)

Another way the solution could be made simpler is to have a constant string to reference output from:

n = int(input())  # Note: 0 < n ≤ 20
S = "1234567890XXXXXXXXXX"
print("*" * (2 * n + 1))
for i in range(0, 10, n):
    print("*" + "*".join(S[x] for x in range(i, i + n)) + "*")
    print("*" * (2 * n + 1))
Thanks for the feedback @Pachichi! Nice to know that you enjoyed it! :) I guess I’ve been hallucinating about the difficulty of my puzzle, and it was never that hard.

Thanks for the feedback @User123! Same thing with @Vincent18, this isn't an "instant" puzzle for top 100 CoC players, which was intended.

About the constant string: I'm not gonna lie, but that constant string [[S]] feels like cheesing the puzzle. Even though my constraint says 0 < n ≤ 20, I really only care about n > 0. I just added an upper boundary because I'm afraid that @5DN1L would get mad at me if I didn't (no offense). I do definitely want to make the upper boundary unknown so players don't cheese the puzzle with a constant string.

Roar, I'm very mad! 😡

Just kidding. 🤪

n > 0 should be naturally assumed based on the visible cases, so it's unnecessary to state that explicitly. Most reverse-mode-only clashes don't specify constraints anyway, so it's perfectly fine for you to remove the whole hint from the stub generator.

Clashes that have other modes enabled are a different story. The upper bounds usually don't matter much for Python coders unless they significantly influence the choice of algorithm. However, they *do* matter for languages that distinguish between types like int and long, where limits can affect implementation details.
Thanks for the clarification @5DN1L! I removed the constraints and decided to change Validator 5 from 19 to 25 (shouldn't really matter). Glad you're not mad at me :):)

@VinceMC I could probably still "cheese" the clash with S = "1234567890"+"X"*100 or some other arbitrarily large number. I don't consider this to be a hacky solution though; as you have pointed out, the puzzle really only cares about n > 0, which is perfectly fine.

Also, I warmly welcome clashes that don't simply take 20s to solve (which is probably why my clashes seem quite a bit harder than usual)! Keep them coming :)

Just wondering, how quickly did you spot the pattern? (instantly, took some counting, or spent about 5 minutes scanning for one) Also, did you solve this in under 15 minutes?

Thanks for your feedback!

Did that in two tries - I got interrupted the first one and switched to something else - it was about 5min but not timed - only at the end of this 1st one did I spot the pattern so not right away - It's only when I got back to it that I saw your request for feedback so I timed it and I was just above 11min for all the tests to pass - I just put the '*' in a banner var before posting the comment but other than that kept it as is: I tried to watch time as I dit it and was not so relaxed (hence the m+=n instead of m=(10+n-1)//n*n).
I wish I did that in a timed one-take, it's just that I recalled your avatar from other contributions so I thought I'd give some feedback.
Maybe the CoC incubation would benefit from time stats hints.
~5min guessed not timed + 11min timed
Thanks so much for the feedback. I will keep your time in mind when considering whether to keep this puzzle or not.
Thanks so much for your help :)

One more thing, is it okay if I don't have an upper boundary for [[n]] because @User123 pointed out that you could add a constant string like:
S = "1234567890XXXXXXXXXX"
and I feel like that's cheesing the puzzle. I want to make the upper boundary unknown so that this approach is avoided.
